title: Plugin Config
description: Configuration for building the agent plugin. Maps abstract role names to concrete model strings and tool identifiers for your environment.

$schema.description: Path to this schema file. Enables editor validation and autocomplete.

target.description: Build target platform. Output goes to out/<target>/.

models.description: Maps model role names used in agent source files to concrete model strings for your provider. Set a value to null to omit the model field (uses the platform default).

fast.description: High-capability model for agents that need strong reasoning (execute, prepare, recon, recon-worker). Examples: 'Claude Opus 4.6 Fast (vercel)', 'opus', null (platform default).

balanced.description: Mid-tier model for evaluation tasks (review). Examples: 'Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview (vercel)', 'sonnet', null (platform default).

auxiliary.description: Cost-effective model for supporting tasks (pre-read, slop-linter). Examples: 'GPT 5.4 (vercel)', 'haiku', null (platform default).

toolGroups.description: Named groups of tool identifiers. Agent source files reference group names, and the build expands them to concrete tool lists. Any name in an agent's tools array that doesn't match a group key passes through as a literal.

additionalProperties.description: Array of concrete tool identifiers for this group.

core.description: Base tools available to most agents (search, read, etc.).

edit.description: File editing tools (only execute agent needs this).

terminal.description: Full terminal access (run, await, kill, get output).

terminal-minimal.description: Limited terminal access (run and get output only).

tasks.description: VS Code task creation and execution.
